title: Friday Sermon 4 April 1997
description:
He answered 'My Lord! Since Thou hast adjudged me as lost I will lure them to the things of the Earth and I will lead them all astray.'

'Except Thy chosen servants from among them.'

'He said 'This is a path leading straight to Me.'

'Surely thou shalt have no power over My servants except such of the erring ones as choose to follow thee.'

'And surely Hell is the rendezvous for them all.'

('The Holy Quran'.  al-Hijr [The Rock City]: 40 - 44).

This subject has been going on for some time for until one gives up the ghost in a state of being among the servants of the Lord of Mercy no guarantee can be given about the life to come.

Satan beckons one from various paths and the way to be safe from the Satanic voices has been taught in لاحول ولا قوة إلا بالله ['There in no power except with Allah'].  There is no power to make me good or to grant me anything except by Allah the Almighty.  If one understands this and has full faith then this is where the worship of Tawhid [oneness of God] begins.  Hazrat Musleh Maud had a vision in which he passed through a perilous path safely saying 'With the blessing and mercy of God'.  I too had seen a similar vision in which the journey was undertaken with the blessing of the prayer 'My Lord! All things serve thee'.  This journey is accomplished safely through one prayer or another.  

The Prophets of God are well aware of the subtleties of the paths.  Muhammad ﷺ the Chosen Prophet would also speak of the paths he had not seen with such clarity that one is amazed.  He would explain in detail how sin is born.  

A Prophet has far greater experience than a common sinner because a Prophet encounters the very subtle paths of the trials champions them with success.  These are known as the subtle paths of Taqwa [awareness of God].  The basic meanings of Taqwa are to safeguard.  A Prophet or a Godly person passes safely through the fine paths and realises afterwards what the perils were!

It is important to understand the subject of Istighfar.  Repentance becomes possible with Istighfar and complete cleanliness is to be kept in view in offering Istaghfar, that is to suppress and demean and denigrate every type of sin so much that it becomes invisible.  The leaves behind which Adam hid were the leaves of Istighfar.  

Our only Jihad is to draw mankind out of sins and include them in the Godly nobility and to grant them the clothes of holiness that are clothes which never tear.  This is a colossal task and we ourselves are sinful and stained.  So we would have to make Istighfar essential to our life.  The meaning of Istighfar is not to utter prayers for repentance, Istighfar means to completely erase the sins so that they can't be seen.  In actual fact to evade the sins completely demands a great calamity.  There are those who strive slowly and gradually and then Allah may bless them with release from sins.  There are those who pass through this calamity in one go and shunning the luxuries of the world they adopt a new hermetic life.  This is the Istighfar which can sometimes burst into flame.

The Promised Messiah (peace be upon him) has spoken of the paths of Istighfar in clear and lucid terms.  

The question arises why was sin created? I would continue this subject and its various aspects in the next sermon.
